While the clan divided into numerous branches, only six families, including the Higaki and Matsuki families, retained the privilege of serving as negi. == Notable Members of the Watarai Clan == * [[Watarai Yukitada]] * [[Watarai Ieyuki]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=必携日本史用語|author=日本史用語研究会|edition=4th ed.|publisher=[[実教出版]]|year=2009|isbn=9784407316599}} * {{Cite book|title=日本史B用語集―A併記|author=全国歴史教育研究協議会|edition=Revised ed.|publisher=[[山川出版社]]|year=2009|isbn=9784634013025}} * {{Cite book|title=神道行法の本―日本の霊統を貫く神祇奉祭の秘事|publisher=[[学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=2005|isbn=9784056037753}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Shake (social class)|Shrine families]] * [[Arakida clan]] * [[Shintō Gobusho]] {{Shintō Sidebar}} {{Shintō-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Mie Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|度会氏|version=103442621|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:度会氏]] [[Category:History of Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Ise Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai Clan]] [[Category:Watarai District]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of Tōkai Region]] [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11486123}}

[[ja:度会氏]] [[ja:度会氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11486123]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{More footnotes needed|date=June 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Clan name = Watarai clan | Crest description = Flower diamond crest, Sixteen-petal chrysanthemum crest | Original surname = [[Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Ise no Ason]] | Ancestor = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}} | Type = [[Shinbetsu]] (descendants of heavenly or earthly deities)
[[Kōbetsu]]
[[Kazoku]] (Barons) | Place of origin = [[Ise Province]] or [[Tanba Province]] | Base = [[Watarai District]], [[Ise Province]] | Key figures = [[Watarai Haruhiko]]
[[Watarai Ieyuki]]
[[Deguchi Nobuyoshi]] | Branches = [[Higaki family]] (shrine family)
Matsuki family (shrine family)
[[Hisamoto family]] (shrine family)
[[Sakume family]] (shrine family)
[[Kawasaki family]] (shrine family)
[[Miyago family]] (shrine family) and others }} The '''Watarai clan''' (度会氏, Watarai-uji) is a Japanese [[:en:Clan|Clan]] whose family name is derived from "Watarai." Until the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], many members of this clan served as [[shrine priests]] (negi) of the [[Toyōke Daijingū|Gekū]] (Outer Shrine) of [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingū]] in a [[:en:Hereditary|Hereditary]] capacity. __TOC__ == Overview == According to widely circulated genealogies, the Watarai clan traces its origins to {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}}, and they are considered descendants of the {{ill|Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}[[Nishikawa Junto]] (ed.), "Watarai Shrine Priest Genealogical Record," in *Shintō Taikei: Ronsetsu-hen, Vol. 7 (Ise Shintō, Part 2)*, Shintō Taikei Compilation Society, 1982, pp. 415–417.. Another theory suggests that the Watarai clan came to Ise with [[Toyouke Ōmikami]], the deity enshrined at the Gekū, originally from [[:en:Tango Province|Tango Province]]Ikegaki Miyuki, "The Enshrinement and Traditions of Toyōke Daijingū," *The Formation and Historical Significance of the Nihon Shoki*, Nenshōsha, 2022, pp. 317–319. (Original article: *Koten to Rekishi*, No. 1, 2018).. The clan served as hereditary priests (negi) of the Gekū from ancient times. During the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] and [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]], they challenged the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Naikū]] (Inner Shrine) and advocated [[Ise faith|Ise Shintō]]. While the clan divided into numerous branches, only six families, including the Higaki and Matsuki families, retained the privilege of serving as negi. == Notable Members of the Watarai Clan == * [[Watarai Yukitada]] * [[Watarai Ieyuki]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=必携日本史用語|author=日本史用語研究会|edition=4th ed.|publisher=[[実教出版]]|year=2009|isbn=9784407316599}} * {{Cite book|title=日本史B用語集―A併記|author=全国歴史教育研究協議会|edition=Revised ed.|publisher=[[山川出版社]]|year=2009|isbn=9784634013025}} * {{Cite book|title=神道行法の本―日本の霊統を貫く神祇奉祭の秘事|publisher=[[学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=2005|isbn=9784056037753}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Shake (social class)|Shrine families]] * [[Arakida clan]] * [[Shintō Gobusho]] {{Shintō Sidebar}} {{Shintō-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Mie Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|度会氏|version=103442621|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:度会氏]] [[Category:History of Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Ise Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai Clan]] [[Category:Watarai District]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of Tōkai Region]] [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11486123}}

[[ja:度会氏]] [[ja:度会氏]] [[ja:度会氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11486123]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{More footnotes needed|date=June 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Clan name = Watarai clan | Crest description = Flower diamond crest, Sixteen-petal chrysanthemum crest | Original surname = [[Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Ise no Ason]] | Ancestor = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}} | Type = [[Shinbetsu]] (descendants of heavenly or earthly deities)
[[Kōbetsu]]
[[Kazoku]] (Barons) | Place of origin = [[Ise Province]] or [[Tanba Province]] | Base = [[Watarai District]], [[Ise Province]] | Key figures = [[Watarai Haruhiko]]
[[Watarai Ieyuki]]
[[Deguchi Nobuyoshi]] | Branches = [[Higaki family]] (shrine family)
Matsuki family (shrine family)
[[Hisamoto family]] (shrine family)
[[Sakume family]] (shrine family)
[[Kawasaki family]] (shrine family)
[[Miyago family]] (shrine family) and others }} The '''Watarai clan''' (度会氏, Watarai-uji) is a Japanese [[:en:Clan|Clan]] whose family name is derived from "Watarai." Until the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], many members of this clan served as [[shrine priests]] (negi) of the [[Toyōke Daijingū|Gekū]] (Outer Shrine) of [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingū]] in a [[:en:Hereditary|Hereditary]] capacity. __TOC__ == Overview == According to widely circulated genealogies, the Watarai clan traces its origins to {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}}, and they are considered descendants of the {{ill|Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}[[Nishikawa Junto]] (ed.), "Watarai Shrine Priest Genealogical Record," in *Shintō Taikei: Ronsetsu-hen, Vol. 7 (Ise Shintō, Part 2)*, Shintō Taikei Compilation Society, 1982, pp. 415–417.. Another theory suggests that the Watarai clan came to Ise with [[Toyouke Ōmikami]], the deity enshrined at the Gekū, originally from [[:en:Tango Province|Tango Province]]Ikegaki Miyuki, "The Enshrinement and Traditions of Toyōke Daijingū," *The Formation and Historical Significance of the Nihon Shoki*, Nenshōsha, 2022, pp. 317–319. (Original article: *Koten to Rekishi*, No. 1, 2018).. The clan served as hereditary priests (negi) of the Gekū from ancient times. During the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] and [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]], they challenged the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Naikū]] (Inner Shrine) and advocated [[Ise faith|Ise Shintō]]. While the clan divided into numerous branches, only six families, including the Higaki and Matsuki families, retained the privilege of serving as negi. == Notable Members of the Watarai Clan == * [[Watarai Yukitada]] * [[Watarai Ieyuki]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=必携日本史用語|author=日本史用語研究会|edition=4th ed.|publisher=[[実教出版]]|year=2009|isbn=9784407316599}} * {{Cite book|title=日本史B用語集―A併記|author=全国歴史教育研究協議会|edition=Revised ed.|publisher=[[山川出版社]]|year=2009|isbn=9784634013025}} * {{Cite book|title=神道行法の本―日本の霊統を貫く神祇奉祭の秘事|publisher=[[学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=2005|isbn=9784056037753}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Shake (social class)|Shrine families]] * [[Arakida clan]] * [[Shintō Gobusho]] {{Shintō Sidebar}} {{Shintō-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Mie Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|度会氏|version=103442621|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:度会氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11486123}} [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Clans of Tōkai Region]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Ise Shrine]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Watarai Clan]] [[Category:Watarai District]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

(Original article: *Koten to Rekishi*, No. 1, 2018).. The clan served as hereditary priests (negi) of the Gekū from ancient times. During the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} and {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}}, they challenged the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Naikū]] (Inner Shrine) and advocated [[Ise faith|Ise Shintō]]. While the clan divided into numerous branches, only six families, including the Higaki and Matsuki families, retained the privilege of serving as negi. == Notable Members of the Watarai Clan == * [[Watarai Yukitada]] * [[Watarai Ieyuki]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=必携日本史用語|author=日本史用語研究会|edition=4th ed.|publisher=[[実教出版]]|year=2009|isbn=9784407316599}} * {{Cite book|title=日本史B用語集―A併記|author=全国歴史教育研究協議会|edition=Revised ed.|publisher=[[山川出版社]]|year=2009|isbn=9784634013025}} * {{Cite book|title=神道行法の本―日本の霊統を貫く神祇奉祭の秘事|publisher=[[学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=2005|isbn=9784056037753}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|WD=Q11588367|lt=Shrine families}} * [[Arakida clan]] * [[Shintō Gobusho]] {{Shintō Sidebar}} {{Shintō-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Mie Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|度会氏|version=103442621|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:度会氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11486123}} [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Clans of Tōkai Region]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Ise Shrine]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Watarai Clan]] [[Category:Watarai District]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:度会氏]] [[ja:度会氏]] [[ja:度会氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11486123]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{More footnotes needed|date=June 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Clan name = Watarai clan | Crest description = Flower diamond crest, Sixteen-petal chrysanthemum crest | Original surname = [[Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Ise no Ason]] | Ancestor = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}} | Type = [[Shinbetsu]] (descendants of heavenly or earthly deities)
[[Kōbetsu]]
[[Kazoku]] (Barons) | Place of origin = [[Ise Province]] or [[Tanba Province]] | Base = [[Watarai District]], [[Ise Province]] | Key figures = [[Watarai Haruhiko]]
[[Watarai Ieyuki]]
[[Deguchi Nobuyoshi]] | Branches = [[Higaki family]] (shrine family)
Matsuki family (shrine family)
[[Hisamoto family]] (shrine family)
[[Sakume family]] (shrine family)
[[Kawasaki family]] (shrine family)
[[Miyago family]] (shrine family) and others }} The '''Watarai clan''' (度会氏, Watarai-uji) is a Japanese {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clan}} whose family name is derived from "Watarai." Until the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, many members of this clan served as [[shrine priests]] (negi) of the [[Toyōke Daijingū|Gekū]] (Outer Shrine) of [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingū]] in a [[:en:Hereditary|Hereditary]] capacity. __TOC__ == Overview == According to widely circulated genealogies, the Watarai clan traces its origins to {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}}, and they are considered descendants of the {{ill|Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}[[Nishikawa Junto]] (ed.), "Watarai Shrine Priest Genealogical Record," in *Shintō Taikei: Ronsetsu-hen, Vol. 7 (Ise Shintō, Part 2)*, Shintō Taikei Compilation Society, 1982, pp. 415–417.. Another theory suggests that the Watarai clan came to Ise with [[Toyouke Ōmikami]], the deity enshrined at the Gekū, originally from {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province}}Ikegaki Miyuki, "The Enshrinement and Traditions of Toyōke Daijingū," *The Formation and Historical Significance of the Nihon Shoki*, Nenshōsha, 2022, pp. 317–319. (Original article: *Koten to Rekishi*, No. 1, 2018).. The clan served as hereditary priests (negi) of the Gekū from ancient times. During the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} and {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}}, they challenged the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Naikū]] (Inner Shrine) and advocated [[Ise faith|Ise Shintō]]. While the clan divided into numerous branches, only six families, including the Higaki and Matsuki families, retained the privilege of serving as negi. == Notable Members of the Watarai Clan == * [[Watarai Yukitada]] * [[Watarai Ieyuki]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=必携日本史用語|author=日本史用語研究会|edition=4th ed.|publisher=[[実教出版]]|year=2009|isbn=9784407316599}} * {{Cite book|title=日本史B用語集―A併記|author=全国歴史教育研究協議会|edition=Revised ed.|publisher=[[山川出版社]]|year=2009|isbn=9784634013025}} * {{Cite book|title=神道行法の本―日本の霊統を貫く神祇奉祭の秘事|publisher=[[学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=2005|isbn=9784056037753}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|WD=Q11588367|lt=Shrine families}} * [[Arakida clan]] * [[Shintō Gobusho]] {{Shintō Sidebar}} {{Shintō-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Mie Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|度会氏|version=103442621|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:度会氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11486123}} [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Clans of Tōkai Region]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Ise Shrine]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Watarai Clan]] [[Category:Watarai District]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

While the clan divided into numerous branches, only six families, including the Higaki and Matsuki families, retained the privilege of serving as negi. == Notable Members of the Watarai Clan == * [[Watarai Yukitada]] * [[Watarai Ieyuki]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=必携日本史用語|author=日本史用語研究会|edition=4th ed.|publisher=[[実教出版]]|year=2009|isbn=9784407316599}} * {{Cite book|title=日本史B用語集―A併記|author=全国歴史教育研究協議会|edition=Revised ed.|publisher=[[山川出版社]]|year=2009|isbn=9784634013025}} * {{Cite book|title=神道行法の本―日本の霊統を貫く神祇奉祭の秘事|publisher=[[学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=2005|isbn=9784056037753}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|WD=Q11588367|lt=Shrine families}} * [[Arakida clan]] * [[Shintō Gobusho]] {{Shintō Sidebar}} {{Shintō-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Mie Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|度会氏|version=103442621|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:度会氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11486123}} [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Clans of Tōkai Region]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Ise Shrine]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Watarai Clan]] [[Category:Watarai District]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

While the clan divided into numerous branches, only six families, including the Higaki and Matsuki families, retained the privilege of serving as negi. == Notable Members of the Watarai Clan == * [[Watarai Yukitada]] * [[Watarai Ieyuki]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=必携日本史用語|author=日本史用語研究会|edition=4th ed.|publisher=[[実教出版]]|year=2009|isbn=9784407316599}} * {{Cite book|title=日本史B用語集―A併記|author=全国歴史教育研究協議会|edition=Revised ed.|publisher=[[山川出版社]]|year=2009|isbn=9784634013025}} * {{Cite book|title=神道行法の本―日本の霊統を貫く神祇奉祭の秘事|publisher=[[学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=2005|isbn=9784056037753}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|WD=Q11588367|lt=Shrine families}} * [[Arakida clan]] * [[Shintō Gobusho]] {{Shintō Sidebar}} {{Shintō-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Mie Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|度会氏|version=103442621|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:度会氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11486123}} [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Clans of Tōkai Region]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Ise Shrine]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Watarai Clan]] [[Category:Watarai District]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:度会氏]] [[ja:度会氏]] [[ja:度会氏]] {{More footnotes needed|date=June 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Clan name = Watarai clan | Crest description = Flower diamond crest, Sixteen-petal chrysanthemum crest | Original surname = [[Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Ise no Ason]] | Ancestor = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}} | Type = [[Shinbetsu]] (descendants of heavenly or earthly deities)
[[Kōbetsu]]
[[Kazoku]] (Barons) | Place of origin = [[Ise Province]] or [[Tanba Province]] | Base = [[Watarai District]], [[Ise Province]] | Key figures = [[Watarai Haruhiko]]
[[Watarai Ieyuki]]
[[Deguchi Nobuyoshi]] | Branches = [[Higaki family]] (shrine family)
Matsuki family (shrine family)
[[Hisamoto family]] (shrine family)
[[Sakume family]] (shrine family)
[[Kawasaki family]] (shrine family)
[[Miyago family]] (shrine family) and others }} The '''Watarai clan''' (度会氏, Watarai-uji) is a Japanese {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clan|1=Clan}} whose family name is derived from "Watarai." Until the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, many members of this clan served as [[shrine priests]] (negi) of the [[Toyōke Daijingū|Gekū]] (Outer Shrine) of [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingū]] in a [[:en:Hereditary|Hereditary]] capacity. __TOC__ == Overview == According to widely circulated genealogies, the Watarai clan traces its origins to {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}}, and they are considered descendants of the {{ill|Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}[[Nishikawa Junto]] (ed.), "Watarai Shrine Priest Genealogical Record," in *Shintō Taikei: Ronsetsu-hen, Vol. 7 (Ise Shintō, Part 2)*, Shintō Taikei Compilation Society, 1982, pp. 415–417.. Another theory suggests that the Watarai clan came to Ise with [[Toyouke Ōmikami]], the deity enshrined at the Gekū, originally from {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province|1=Tango Province}}Ikegaki Miyuki, "The Enshrinement and Traditions of Toyōke Daijingū," *The Formation and Historical Significance of the Nihon Shoki*, Nenshōsha, 2022, pp. 317–319. (Original article: *Koten to Rekishi*, No. 1, 2018).. The clan served as hereditary priests (negi) of the Gekū from ancient times. During the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} and {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, they challenged the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Naikū]] (Inner Shrine) and advocated [[Ise faith|Ise Shintō]]. While the clan divided into numerous branches, only six families, including the Higaki and Matsuki families, retained the privilege of serving as negi. == Notable Members of the Watarai Clan == * [[Watarai Yukitada]] * [[Watarai Ieyuki]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=必携日本史用語|author=日本史用語研究会|edition=4th ed.|publisher=[[実教出版]]|year=2009|isbn=9784407316599}} * {{Cite book|title=日本史B用語集―A併記|author=全国歴史教育研究協議会|edition=Revised ed.|publisher=[[山川出版社]]|year=2009|isbn=9784634013025}} * {{Cite book|title=神道行法の本―日本の霊統を貫く神祇奉祭の秘事|publisher=[[学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=2005|isbn=9784056037753}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|WD=Q11588367|lt=Shrine families|1=Shake (social class)}} * [[Arakida clan]] * [[Shintō Gobusho]] {{Shintō Sidebar}} {{Shintō-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Mie Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|度会氏|version=103442621|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:度会氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11486123}} [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Clans of Tōkai Region]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Ise Shrine]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Watarai Clan]] [[Category:Watarai District]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]
